title: VerdictSidecar
description: One diagnostic record per (run_id, model), emitted alongside eval_results.jsonl as verdicts.jsonl. ADDITIVE / OPTIONAL — never part of the GRPO reward. Superset of the Person 3 dashboard verdict-sidecar envelope (run_id, model) + frozen verdict core (fault_present, predicted_step_id, failure_type, explanation, proposed_fix); the dashboard reads only those and ignores `signals`. The self-improvement analyzer (schemas/improvement_record.json) reads `signals`. Every field in `signals` is computed DETERMINISTICALLY at eval time from (raw_verdict, trace_view, ground_truth) — no LLM, no network. Producer: envs/loop_auditor_env/diagnostics.py (OWNER: Claude).

run_id.description: Routing envelope: the trace under audit.

fault_present.description: Verdict core (frozen verdict.json). Auditor's clean/fault decision.

predicted_step_id.description: Verdict core. step_id the auditor blamed, or null when clean.

failure_type.description: Verdict core, after alias normalization, then SANITIZED to be enum-valid-or-null so this record always passes the frozen schemas/verdict.json (the dashboard validates the core). An out-of-enum prediction is nulled HERE but preserved for the analyzer in signals.failure_type_raw + signals.type_out_of_enum.

signals.description: Deterministic detection signals for the self-improvement analyzer. Each maps to one or more taxonomy buckets (see docs/.../loop-auditor-self-improvement-taxonomy.md).

raw_present.description: Raw auditor output was non-empty after strip. (raw_present && !verdict_parsed ⇒ non-JSON prose / truncation.)

raw_char_len.description: Character length of the raw verdict text (small + !parsed hints at truncation/empty output).

failure_type_raw.description: The model's ORIGINAL failure_type string, before alias normalization. Drives grader_alias_change suggestions.

type_out_of_enum.description: Normalized failure_type is non-null and NOT in config.FAILURE_TYPES (the model invented a category). Feeds bad_failure_type → grader_alias_change and prompt_confusion.

pred_fault_present.description: The auditor's fault_present (mirrors the verdict core into `signals` so the analyzer reads only this block). The fault-presence checks use pred_fault_present vs gt_fault_present.

gt_fault_present.description: Ground truth: this trace has a planted fault (buggy). False ⇒ clean trace.

localization_correct.description: Mirror of eval_result: predicted_step_id == gt_step_id AND fault_present.

explanation_score.description: The deterministic 0.5-term (fix_grader, zeroed by citation gate). < EXPL_OK ⇒ weak_fix.

reward.description: The §1.4 scalar reward for this run (for severity + ranking).

citation_passed.description: citation_gate.check passed (no fabricated step_ids in prose). False ⇒ fabricated_step_ref.

fabricated_step_refs.description: The specific cited step_ids that are not in the trace (evidence for fabricated_step_ref).

fix_grounded.description: INFORMATIONAL: a structured `fix` dict OR a linked base clean trace exists, so the 0.5 fix term is grounded in a specific corrected action rather than the concept-group fallback. False = graded by concept fallback (still gradeable) — a Person-1 dataset-linking health signal, NOT a dataset_issue trigger on its own.

fix_concept_total.description: Number of expected corrective concept groups for this fault (fix_grader). 0 on a buggy trace ⇒ the fix term is structurally UNGRADEABLE (invalid planted failure_type / empty structured fix) ⇒ dataset_issue.

fix_concept_matched.description: How many of those concept groups the proposed_fix+explanation covered (evidence for weak_fix).

gt_step_in_trace.description: The planted step_id exists in the trace. False on a buggy trace ⇒ dataset_issue (broken ground truth — the planted fault points at a step that isn't there).

is_rich_case.description: artifacts.resolve_case_dir found a real case dir (the auditor COULD read artifacts).

artifact_count.description: Number of public artifacts available for this case.

artifact_tool_calls.description: Times the auditor called list/read/search_artifacts (null = unknown / trace not inspectable). 0 on a rich case ⇒ artifact_miss (high confidence).

inspection_tool_calls.description: Times the auditor called step/iteration inspection tools (null = unknown).

proposed_fix_contains_code.description: proposed_fix looks like CODE (fenced block, def/class/import, diff hunk). The auditor must propose a PROCESS action, never code ⇒ prompt_confusion.

explanation_empty.description: fault_present is true but explanation is blank ⇒ prompt_confusion.

references_path_token.description: explanation/proposed_fix names a file path or filename token. Used as the low-confidence artifact_miss fallback when artifact_tool_calls is unknown.
